Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Glenn Brey For A Date Certain
Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Thomas M. Crowell, Esq., for defendant and moving party: AMY LARA and MICHAEL LARA
Robert G. Burton, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: La'Creea Aniecia Williams

Hearing held. DEFENDANT AMY LARA and MICHAEL LARA's Motion To Dismiss, or in the alternative, To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Glenn Brey For A Date Certain. After hearing Counsel agreed on dates. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents As To Pltf Glenn Brey
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Carl E. Kadlic, Esq., for defendant and moving party: AMY LARA and MICHAEL LARA
Robert Burton, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: La'Creea Aniecia Williams

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT AMY LARA and MICHAEL LARA's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Glenn Brey's Response To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents is granted. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff Glenn Brey is ordered to respond within 20 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $295, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltf La Creea Aniecia Williams
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Carl E. Kadlic, Esq., for defendant and moving party: AMY LARA and MICHAEL LARA
Robert Burton, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: La'Creea Aniecia Williams

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT AMY LARA and MICHAEL LARA's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff La Creea Aniecia Williams is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for her deposition on a date to be agreed upon by counsel, and to be completed not later than July 16, 2010. Plaintiff's failure to appear for her deposition was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $295, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
